explanations.1: This special comment tells Docker to use an advanced version of the Dockerfile syntax that includes experimental features.
explanations.3: This creates a temporary intermediate image called 'builder' that we'll use to install dependencies, but won't include in the final image.
explanations.13: The --mount flag creates a temporary cache folder during this build step so pip doesn't re-download packages if you rebuild the image.
explanations.14: The --prefix flag installs the Python package to a specific folder (/python) instead of the default system location, making it easy to copy later.
explanations.16: This starts a fresh final image from a clean Python base; the previous 'builder' image is discarded to keep the final image smaller.
explanations.22: This tells Python where to find the installed packages (/python/lib/python3.14/site-packages) since we installed them to a custom location.
explanations.23: This adds the /python/bin directory to the system PATH so commands installed by pip can be run directly.
explanations.28: The /usr/sbin/nologin shell prevents anyone from logging in as this user, even if they had the password, which is a security best practice.
explanations.39: This makes the container automatically run the markitdown tool when started, treating the container like a standalone command-line application.
